question.text: Migration across the value chain occurs when:

answer.text: A company diversifies into unrelated businesses
Industries at one end of the value chain gain at the cost of those at the other end due to a phenomenon
A company changes its supply chain logistics provider
Market indices migrate to higher levels

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Value chain migration happens when one part of the value chain gains at the expense of another. Example: cheap telecom services benefited digital content providers at the cost of telecom companies themselves. **The Trap:** This is about systematic shifts in where value is captured in a supply chain, not individual company decisions or market movements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Value chain migration tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 6. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Identify which part of the value chain captures the most value and whether this is shifting. Companies in the gaining part will outperform.
